brief.text: Morgan Rogers is the headline act this morning, with both the Guardian and BBC reporting Arsenal have made the Villa man their number one target, and Fabrizio Romano's podcast backing it up with talk that the club "keep working on" the deal, so this one has real legs even if it is not yet over the line. Keep half an eye on Folarin Balogun too, as David Ornstein says the academy product is expected to leave Monaco this summer with a roughly 50 million euro price tag, though that one is still developing rather than done. The Julian Alvarez chatter is firmly in rumour territory despite his Atletico exit noises, so treat the 431 million euro nonsense with the pinch of salt it deserves. Elsewhere it is feel-good World Cup stuff, with David Raya beaming about his Golden Glove season and Declan Rice earning warm words from Thomas Tuchel, which is exactly what you want from your spine heading into a big summer.
